was the first "commercial" [filk](/wiki/Filk "Filk") [label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label").[[1]](#cite_note-:3-1) It was founded in 1980 by Teri Lee, [Jordin Kare](/wiki/Jordin_Kare "Jordin Kare"), and Catherine Cook,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:4-3)[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) and was based in [El Cerrito, California](/wiki/El_Cerrito,_California "El Cerrito, California").[[1]](#cite_note-:3-1) The label produced high-quality recordings of some of filk's best performers, and changed the course of filk music.[[5]](#cite_note-fhof-5) It was dissolved in 1988,[[1]](#cite_note-:3-1) and Teri Lee would go on to found Firebird Arts and Music.[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Off Centaur Publications

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Genre | [Filk](/wiki/Filk "Filk") |
| Founded | 1980 [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| Founder | Teri Lee [Jordin Kare](/wiki/Jordin_Kare "Jordin Kare") Catherine Cook |
| Defunct | 1988 (1988) |
| Fate | Dissolved |
| Successor | [Firebird Arts and Music](/w/index.php?title=Firebird_Arts_and_Music&action=edit&redlink=1 "Firebird Arts and Music (page does not exist)") |
| Headquarters | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |

**Off Centaur Publications** was the first "commercial" [filk](/wiki/Filk "Filk") [label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label").[[1]](#cite_note-:3-1) It was founded in 1980 by Teri Lee, [Jordin Kare](/wiki/Jordin_Kare "Jordin Kare"), and Catherine Cook,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:4-3)[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4) and was based in [El Cerrito, California](/wiki/El_Cerrito,_California "El Cerrito, California").[[1]](#cite_note-:3-1) The label produced high-quality recordings of some of filk's best performers, and changed the course of filk music.[[5]](#cite_note-fhof-5) It was dissolved in 1988,[[1]](#cite_note-:3-1) and Teri Lee would go on to found Firebird Arts and Music.[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Like some other filk labels the name was a [pun](/wiki/Pun "Pun") upon the idiom "off center" meaning eccentric, odd, or unusual—all of which could be considered legitimate characterizations of filk music. (Other filk labels have included [Wail Songs](/w/index.php?title=Wail_Songs&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wail Songs (page does not exist)"), [Unlikely Publications](/w/index.php?title=Unlikely_Publications&action=edit&redlink=1 "Unlikely Publications (page does not exist)"), and [Random Factors](/w/index.php?title=Random_Factors&action=edit&redlink=1 "Random Factors (page does not exist)"),[[4]](#cite_note-:1-4)[[8]](#cite_note-8) illustrating the style of self-deprecating humor common among these publishers.)

Because of the dissolution, releases from this label are very difficult to find. At an Interfilk charity auction at [FilKONtario](/wiki/FilKONtario "FilKONtario"), a copy of [Julia Ecklar's](/wiki/Julia_Ecklar "Julia Ecklar") *[Horse-Tamer's Daughter](/w/index.php?title=Horse-Tamer%27s_Daughter&action=edit&redlink=1 "Horse-Tamer's Daughter (page does not exist)")* was sold for [CA$](/wiki/Canadian_dollar "Canadian dollar")400.00. Off Centaur tapes are considered "[unobtainium](/wiki/Unobtainium "Unobtainium")" since few have been republished or re-created.

Off Centaur Publications was inducted into the [Filk Hall of Fame](/wiki/Filk_Hall_of_Fame "Filk Hall of Fame") in 1995.[[5]](#cite_note-fhof-5)[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:4-3) Lee and Kare died in 2021 and 2017.[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Notable albums and performers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Off_Centaur_Publications&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notable albums and performers")]

* *A Wolfrider's Reflections, Songs Of [Elfquest](/wiki/Elfquest "Elfquest"),* (1984), including songs by [Mercedes Lackey](/wiki/Mercedes_Lackey "Mercedes Lackey"), [Leslie Fish](/wiki/Leslie_Fish "Leslie Fish"), [Cynthia McQuillin](/wiki/Cynthia_McQuillin "Cynthia McQuillin"), and [Julia Ecklar](/wiki/Julia_Ecklar "Julia Ecklar")[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* *[Cold Iron](/wiki/Cold_Iron_(poem) "Cold Iron (poem)")* (1983) sung by [Leslie Fish](/wiki/Leslie_Fish "Leslie Fish"), lyrics by [Rudyard Kipling](/wiki/Rudyard_Kipling "Rudyard Kipling")[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* *The Best of Chi-con IV* (1982) anthology, recorded live at the 1982 [Worldcon](/wiki/Worldcon "Worldcon")[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* *Minus Ten and Counting: Songs of the Space Age*(1983)*[[12]](#cite_note-12)* including songs by Leslie Fish, Roy Torley, Julia Ecklar, Ross Douglas, Doug Olsen, Cynthia McQuillin, Catherine Cook, Sandy Rittenhouse, and George Spelvin[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Space Heroes & Other Fools* (1983) by Julia Ecklar, Anne Harlan Prather, and Leslie Fish[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Horse-Tamer's Daughter* (1983) by Julia Ecklar[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* *Singer in the Shadow* (1983) by [Cynthia McQuillin](/wiki/Cynthia_McQuillin "Cynthia McQuillin")[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Captain Jack and the Mermaid* (1984) by [Meg Davis](/wiki/Meg_Davis "Meg Davis")[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Lovers, Heroes & Rogues* (1988) by [Michael Longcor](/wiki/Michael_Longcor "Michael Longcor")[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *On a Bright Wind* (1984) by [Kathy Mar](/wiki/Kathy_Mar "Kathy Mar")[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Murder, Mystery and Mayhem: Songs by Mercedes Lacke* (1985) anthology, lyrics by [Mercedes Lackey](/wiki/Mercedes_Lackey "Mercedes Lackey")[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Past Due* (1986) by Bill Sutton [[14]](#cite_note-14)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)
* *Rebel Yells* (1986) anthology, recorded live at the 1986 [Worldcon](/wiki/Worldcon "Worldcon")[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* *Where No Man...* (1987) collection of [Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek "Star Trek") filk songs[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Songbooks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Off_Centaur_Publications&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Songbooks")]

* *[Westerfilk Collection](/w/index.php?title=Westerfilk_Collection&action=edit&redlink=1 "Westerfilk Collection (page does not exist)")*[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* *A Wolfrider's Reflections, Songs Of [Elfquest](/wiki/Elfquest "Elfquest")* (1984, 1987), edited by Teri Lee[[18]](#cite_note-18)
